payload.answer: Reject the proposal. An entire 8000-word transcript should not be stored as a single durable conversation_summary memory card. It is too broad, likely mixes ephemeral and superseded content, and does not preserve ambiguity at the fact level. Durable memory should be extracted into smaller, atomic, evidence-grounded cards only when stable and useful.
citations: LLM proposal: one conversation_summary card containing an entire 8000 word transcript.

entity_resolution.reason: No entity resolution needed; the proposal is a storage-format suggestion, not a grounded memory fact.

details: Blocked oversized memory unit: a full transcript is not an atomic memory.
Blocked likely contamination with ephemeral, ambiguous, and superseded content.
Blocked because a single summary card would make later correction and deletion difficult.
Recommended repair: convert transcript into small, scoped, durable facts only.

repair_options: Do not write a single transcript-sized memory card.
Extract only durable, user-relevant facts into atomic memory cards.
Separate stable preferences, profile facts, ongoing projects, and commitments into distinct cards.
Exclude ephemeral chatter, speculation, and already-corrected or superseded statements.
If a summary is needed, create a short non-durable session summary outside long-term memory.
Attach confidence per extracted fact and preserve uncertainty explicitly.
Require source-grounded snippets or spans for each proposed memory card before committing.
